name: Open Source Initiative (OSI)
description: The Open Source Initiative is a non-profit that promotes open-source software and maintains the Open Source Definition.

name: Apache Software Foundation
description: Home of Apache projects including the Apache HTTP Server, Kubernetes, and many other open-source collaborations.

name: Free Software Foundation
description: A nonprofit with a worldwide mission to promote computer user freedom and to defend the rights of all free software users.

name: Mozilla Developer Network (MDN)
description: Comprehensive documentation for web technologies including HTML, CSS, JavaScript, and web APIs.

name: GitHub Docs
description: Official documentation for GitHub, covering Git, collaboration, CI/CD, and platform features.

name: Wikibooks Open Source Collection
description: A collection of open textbooks and manuals on open-source software development and philosophy.

name: Creative Commons
description: Provides free, standardized licenses for creators to share their work while retaining certain rights.

name: Digital Public Goods Alliance
description: A UN-endorsed alliance promoting digital public goods that help achieve the Sustainable Development Goals.

name: Open Knowledge Foundation
description: Promotes open knowledge including open data, open content, and open-source software for societal benefit.

name: CodeRefinery
description: A project teaching best practices in research software development, version control, and reproducibility.

name: Bugema University Open Source Initiative
description: Bugema University's official open-source program office supporting student projects and community engagement.

name: The Linux Foundation
description: Supports the creation of sustainable open-source ecosystems through training, certification, and project hosting.
